name: 📡 OVERVIEW — Workflows That Communicate With Users

parameters.content:
# 📡 Mutiso.AI — Workflows That Communicate With Users
Every n8n workflow that sends a message to (or converses with) a real contractor/foreman, in one place. **Reference only — this workflow is inactive and never runs.** Full technical detail on each one lives in `n8n/README.md` in the repo.

**Channel:** WhatsApp via Evolution API unless noted otherwise. Last updated 2026-07-18.
## 🔔 Event-Triggered Alerts — fire the instant something happens (Postgres trigger → webhook), no cron involved

### 1. Severe Incident Alert
**Trigger:** `incident_log` medium/high severity → webhook
**To:** Site owner
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Severity, category, description, workers involved, date — sent immediately after a serious incident is logged.

### 2. Permit Request Notification
**Trigger:** New `work_permit` row → webhook
**To:** Site owner (skipped if they requested it themselves)
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Permit type, who requested it, validity window — a decision is waiting on the owner.

### 3. Variation Order Raised
**Trigger:** New `variation_order` row → webhook
**To:** The site's assigned **foreman**, not the owner (raising a VO is owner-only, so this reaches whoever is actually affected on site)
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Title, cost impact (KES), time impact, description.

### 4. Foreman Invite Email
**Trigger:** New `invites` row with an email → webhook
**To:** The invited foreman's email
**Channel:** Gmail
**Says:** Welcome message with their join link. Replaced the old fire-and-forget browser call — now there's exactly one delivery path, not two competing ones.
## 📅 Scheduled Digests — cron-triggered, staggered across Mon–Thu mornings so a contractor never gets several at once

### 5. Certification & Insurance Expiring Digest
**When:** Daily, 07:00 (Africa/Nairobi)
**To:** Every contractor with something expiring
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Worker/equipment certs + subcontractor insurance expiring within 30 days or already expired, across all their active sites — one combined message.

### 6. Weekly Payroll Reminder
**When:** Mondays, 07:00
**To:** Contractor
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Which of their sites logged attendance last week but still have no payroll run generated.

### 7. Weekly Site Digest
**When:** Mondays, 08:00 (30 min after payroll reminder)
**To:** Contractor — **one message per site**, not combined
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Last week's attendance, diary entries, defects opened/closed, milestones completed. Skipped for a site with nothing to report.

### 8. Budget Overrun Alert
**When:** Tuesdays, 07:00
**To:** Contractor, one combined message
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Every (site, category) budget line at ≥90% spent, worst first, KES formatted.

### 9. Tool & Maintenance Digest
**When:** Wednesdays, 07:00
**To:** Contractor, one combined message
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** Tools checked out >7 days with no return (a heuristic — no due-date field exists yet), plus equipment maintenance due within 7 days.

### 10. Waste Disposal Compliance Digest
**When:** Thursdays, 07:00
**To:** Contractor, one combined message
**Channel:** WhatsApp
**Says:** The week's waste log entries grouped by site/type/disposal method, quantities summed.
## 💬 Conversational — the one workflow a user actually talks *to*, rather than just receiving alerts from

### 11. WhatsApp Contractor Chatbot
**Trigger:** Inbound WhatsApp message (Evolution API webhook)
**To/From:** Any contractor, free-form questions
**Channel:** WhatsApp, OpenAI gpt-4o-mini + persistent chat memory
**Says:** Answers real questions about their own sites — attendance, deliveries, usage, inventory, incidents, defects, diary, progress, payroll, visitors, certifications, permits, budget. Identity is resolved from the sender's phone number server-side before any AI runs; an unrecognized number gets a canned reply and never reaches the LLM.

### 11b. Bot Query Data
*(internal helper, not user-facing)*

The sub-workflow #11's AI Agent calls as its tool — runs `bot_query_site_data()` via a proven Postgres credential. Built as a separate sub-workflow because direct HTTP/Code tool nodes hit real bugs on this n8n instance.
